preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiceless palato-alveolar affricate is a process of assibilation and palatalization, where a rhotic sound strengthens into an obstruent with a fricative release.

examples.note: In some rural highland dialects, rhotics can be assibilated and sometimes devoiced toward an affricate or fricative articulation.
The historical palatalized rhotic (rz) assibilated into a retroflex/palatal sound, which in some clusters (like after voiceless stops) is realized as a voiceless fricative or affricate.

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
Swan, O. E. (2002). A Grammar of Contemporary Polish.
